title: crocus biflorus subsp. weldenii
intro: Crocus biflorus subsp. weldenii, also known as Welden's crocus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Crocus, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to contain colchicine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antineoplastic properties. The health benefits of Crocus biflorus subsp. weldenii include its potential use in treating gout and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its possible role in preventing the growth of certain types of cancer cells. The plant is typically harvested for its bulbs, which are used to prepare herbal remedies such as tinctures and infusions, although caution should be exercised due to the potential toxicity of colchicine.
